It is used by half a million public servants and policymakers across 170 countries. Providing governments with the tools, the skills and the networks they need to become more effective and tech enabled organizations. Apolitical is a strategic partner to governments helping them prepare their workforces for the future. We achieve this by upskilling government workforces through short online courses focused on government priorities, enabling them to find and share best practice through peer communities and building innovative tools designed for and with governments. Anything with a square bracket is optional, so you don't need to care about it. In text split, I'm going to be doing this person, comma, and then I have a column delimiter. Delimiter is a word we use in tech to mean a character or a group of characters that splits up text. In this case, we're going to be doing a space, and whenever you do text inside a formula in Excel, you need to start and end with these speech marks, double quotations. Press enter, and you get it appearing like this. Then you can drag it down, and let me know in the chat, give me a reaction if you like that one. That is called text split. It's available to Excel 365 and Excel 2024. Here's a thing that happens a lot with us, which is U .S. dates. How annoying are these? Why did Americans have to do this to dates? Everyone else in the world does dates in one way, whereas Americans decided to do it completely different. What you can do is you can select a column. This is a very hidden feature. It's in the data tab again. You have this thing called text to columns, and you can click on that. The next one is a question that came in the pre -chat, which is what happens if you have some of these hidden extra lines that you want to reduce or if you have extra spaces. You have two functions. One is called equals trim. Trim will remove any white space before or after text, and in the middle, it will reduce it to one like that. Very simple. Equals trim. Click on the cell. If you want to remove a new line, you have one called clean. In a perfect world, I wish you had one function that did both, but you don't. Equals clean will remove that. You might want to add a space, of course, because it's not replacing it space, but it is able to do that. Great. That is what I wanted to cover in this one. And I can choose to highlight the text that says Microsoft and I can choose the color like yellow and press okay. And now every time it says Microsoft, it's automatically highlighted. For example, here going to select the sales column and I can go to conditional formatting. Essentially, conditional formatting as a concept is how you make cells look different based on what's in them without writing a single formula. Super, super easy to understand. But what I can do is I can go to color scales and I can just choose this and then it makes the lowest numbers in red, the highest numbers in green and anything in that scale like a heat map. And if you have something that is really, really high, like let me add in a few zeros, you're going to see this one become very, very green and all the rest become something else because this is a much higher order of magnitude. Now, last conditional formatting thing I'll show you also with lots of numbers, select the cells, go to conditional formatting and you have data bars. Check this out. You can do a bar chart inside the cell where the highest number is filled up all the way till the end. People like that one and just, and then zeros filled up like that.
